items.prompt: Editorial portrait illustration of Sherlock Holmes — At 221B Baker Street. Style: Japanese sumi-e ink wash, minimal brush strokes, negative space, single subject on rice paper. Composition: single figure in portrait crop, rich tonal depth, strong focal point. No text, no letters, no watermark, no frame, no border. Public-domain source: Arthur Conan Doyle 1887. Collectible trading-card artwork aesthetic.

Editorial portrait illustration of The Hound of the Baskervilles — On the Moor. Style: Victorian steel engraving, fine crosshatch line work, sepia monochrome, 1880s periodical aesthetic. Composition: single figure in portrait crop, rich tonal depth, strong focal point. No text, no letters, no watermark, no frame, no border. Public-domain source: Arthur Conan Doyle 1902. Collectible trading-card artwork aesthetic.

Editorial portrait illustration of The Phantom of the Opera — Behind the Mask. Style: Modern cel-shaded anime, flat color fills with hard edges, expressive character design, studio-quality animation cel. Composition: single figure in portrait crop, rich tonal depth, strong focal point. No text, no letters, no watermark, no frame, no border. Public-domain source: Gaston Leroux 1910. Collectible trading-card artwork aesthetic.

Editorial portrait illustration of Medusa — The Serpent-Haired Gorgon. Style: Baroque oil painting, Caravaggio-style chiaroscuro, warm amber glow against velvet black, visible brushwork. Composition: single figure in portrait crop, rich tonal depth, strong focal point. No text, no letters, no watermark, no frame, no border. Public-domain source: Hesiod c.700 BCE. Collectible trading-card artwork aesthetic.
